Use normal tool calls instead when: single tool call with no processing, you need to see the full result and apply complex reasoning, or the task requires interactive user input.

Available via `from hermes_tools import ...`:

  write_file(path: str, content: str) -> dict
    Always overwrites the entire file.
  search_files(pattern: str, target="content", path=".", file_glob=None, limit=50) -> dict
    target: "content" (search inside files) or "files" (find files by name). Returns {"matches": [...]}
  patch(path: str, old_string: str, new_string: str, replace_all: bool = False) -> dict
    Replaces old_string with new_string in the file.
  terminal(command: str, timeout=None, workdir=None) -> dict
    Foreground only (no background/pty). Returns {"output": "...", "exit_code": N}

Limits: 5-minute timeout, 50KB stdout cap, max 50 tool calls per script. terminal() is foreground-only (no background or pty).

Print your final result to stdout. Use Python stdlib (json, re, math, csv, datetime, collections, etc.) for processing between tool calls.

     3|  "idea": "# Post-Validation Narrative Synthesis\n\n## Outcome\n\nImplement a post-validation final-message system for VibeComfy agent edits. The final user-facing message should be LLM-written from high-quality structured context, but it must be generated after execution and validation so it describes what actually happened, not what the executor intended.\n\n## Scope\n\nIn scope:\n\n- Add a structured narrative context artifact for each agent-edit turn.\n- Build that context after execution, response outcome selection, and validation facts are available.\n- Use the context to synthesize final messages for success, success-with-warnings, clarify, noop, blocked, and failed outcomes.\n- Keep executor-authored messages as debug/input material, but stop treating them as final truth.\n- Add deterministic validation of synthesized messages so they cannot contradict core facts such as graph changed/unchanged, landed operation count, validation failure, or clarification outcome.\n- Add deterministic fallback messages if narrative synthesis fails or violates constraints.\n- Persist the narrative context, request, response, and validation result beside existing turn artifacts.\n- Update tests and, where practical, live-agentic harness checks so message quality is assessed against actual artifacts.\n\nOut of scope:\n\n- Reworking research, planning, or execution agent behavior except where required to expose data to the narrative context.\n- Replacing the current response envelope contract.\n- Changing unrelated prompt policy or ComfyUI node validation policy.\n- Broad UI redesign.\n\n## Locked Decisions\n\n- Final messages remain LLM-authored when possible.\n- The final-message LLM is a post-fact narrator, not the executor.\n- The narrator may only rely on structured context assembled from persisted artifacts and validated state.\n- The executor's `message` should be preserved as an input/debug artifact, but should not directly become the public final message for failed or w... --skip-git-repo-check...","raw_output_file":"execute_v1_raw.txt","result":"error","step":"execute","timestamp":"2026-06-30T23:19:16Z"}],"idea":"# Post-Validation Narrative Synthesis\n\n## Outcome\n\nImplement a post-validation final-message system for VibeComfy agent edits. The final user-facing message should be LLM-written from high-quality structured context, but it must be generated after execution and validation so it describes what actually happened, not what the executor intended.\n\n## Scope\n\nIn scope:\n\n- Add a structured narrative context artifact for each agent-edit turn.\n- Build that context after execution, response outcome selection, and validation facts are available.\n- Use the context to synthesize final messages for success, success-with-warnings, clarify, noop, blocked, and failed outcomes.\n- Keep executor-authored messages as debug/input material, but stop treating them as final truth.\n- Add deterministic validation of synthesized messages so they cannot contradict core facts such as graph changed/unchanged, landed operation count, validation failure, or clarification outcome.\n- Add deterministic fallback messages if narrative synthesis fails or violates constraints.\n- Persist the narrative context, request, response, and validation result beside existing turn artifacts.\n- Update tests and, where practical, live-agentic harness checks so message quality is assessed against actual artifacts.\n\nOut of scope:\n\n- Reworking research, planning, or execution agent behavior except where required to expose data to the narrative context.\n- Replacing the current response envelope contract.\n- Changing unrelated prompt policy or ComfyUI node validation policy.\n- Broad UI redesign.\n\n## Locked Decisions\n\n- Final messages remain LLM-authored when possible.\n- The final-message LLM is a post-fact narrator, not the executor.\n- The narrator may only rely on structured context assembled from persisted artifacts and validated state.\n- The executor's `message` should be preserved as an input/debug artifact, but should not directly become the public final message for failed or warning cases, and should be diff-checked for success cases.\n- `edit_response_contract.py` and `edit_humanize.py` are likely touchpoints, but the implementation should preserve existing response contracts.\n- If narrative synthesis fails, the turn itself should not fail; use a deterministic fallback message.\n\n## Open Questions For The Planner\n\n- What exact state is available at response-construction time today, and what needs to be plumbed from validation/diagnostics?\n- Should the narrative LLM call be mandatory for all outcomes, or should clean simple successes use deterministic summaries unless complexity/warnings justify LLM synthesis?\n- What is the cheapest reliable model/provider path for the narrative call, and how should provider failures be isolated from edit success?\n- Where should message-quality checks live in the live agentic harness, and how strict should they be initially?\n\n## Constraints\n\n- Do not make final messaging able to change the graph or candidate.\n- Do not let message synthesis introduce a new hard failure mode for otherwise valid edits.\n- Do not expose internal gate jargon in the public message unless it is translated into user-actionable language.\n- Preserve existing public response fields unless there is a deliberate migration path.\n- Keep artifacts compact enough that normal turns do not become noisy or expensive.\n- Respect dirty worktree constraints; do not revert unrelated local changes.\n\n## Done Criteria\n\n- A new persisted narrative context artifact exists for agent-edit turns and includes at least: task, route/outcome, graph_changed, landed operations, rejected operations or diagnostics when available, validation/gate summary, selected precedent/plan summary if available, apply/queue eligibility, and recovery hint or warning data.\n- Final success messages are grounded in actual landed operations and do not claim no-op when operations landed.\n- Final failure/blocked/noop messages identify the phase/root cause and whether retry/user action is appropriate.\n- Deterministic contradiction checks prevent messages from claiming edits happened when `graph_changed=false`, claiming validation passed when validation failed, or asking no question for clarify outcomes.\n- Deterministic fallback messages cover success, clarify, noop, blocked, provider failure, malformed model response, validation failure, and refused emit.\n- Unit tests cover the contradiction guards and fallback behavior.\n- At least one existing live-agentic failure artifact is used as a regression fixture or equivalent test case for misleading executor narratives.\n- The standard relevant test suite passes.\n\n## Touchpoints\n\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/edit_batch_loop_intro.py`\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/edit_response_contract.py`\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/edit_humanize.py`\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/diagnostics.py`\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/contracts.py`\n- `tests/live_agentic_harness/`\n- Existing turn artifacts under `out/editor_sessions/.../turns/...`\n\n## Anti-Scope\n\n- Do not solve all agentic eval failures in this sprint.\n- Do not rewrite the whole research-plan-execute architecture.\n- Do not add broad prompt instructions that make the executor more verbose instead of fixing final-message grounding.\n- Do not hide failures by making messages vague.\n\n## Prep Selection\n\nOverall plan difficulty: 4/5; selected profile: partnered-4; because the main risk is an architectural/message-contract plan that can pass ordinary tests while still producing misleading user-facing output.\n\nPlanning complexity: full; default critique/gate/review is appropriate because this crosses response construction, artifacts, and eval semantics.\n\nDepth: high; the planner needs to trace current state flow and design a clean integration without Outcome\n\nImplement a post-validation final-message system for VibeComfy agent edits. The final user-facing message should be LLM-written from high-quality structured context, but it must be generated after execution and validation so it describes what actually happened, not what the executor intended.\n\n## Scope\n\nIn scope:\n\n- Add a structured narrative context artifact for each agent-edit turn.\n- Build that context after execution, response outcome selection, and validation facts are available.\n- Use the context to synthesize final messages for success, success-with-warnings, clarify, noop, blocked, and failed outcomes.\n- Keep executor-authored messages as debug/input material, but stop treating them as final truth.\n- Add deterministic validation of synthesized messages so they cannot contradict core facts such as graph changed/unchanged, landed operation count, validation failure, or clarification outcome.\n- Add deterministic fallback messages if narrative synthesis fails or violates constraints.\n- Persist the narrative context, request, response, and validation result beside existing turn artifacts.\n- Update tests and, where practical, live-agentic harness checks so message quality is assessed against actual artifacts.\n\nOut of scope:\n\n- Reworking research, planning, or execution agent behavior except where required to expose data to the narrative context.\n- Replacing the current response envelope contract.\n- Changing unrelated prompt policy or ComfyUI node validation policy.\n- Broad UI redesign.\n\n## Locked Decisions\n\n- Final messages remain LLM-authored when possible.\n- The final-message LLM is a post-fact narrator, not the executor.\n- The narrator may only rely on structured context assembled from persisted artifacts and validated state.\n- The executor's `message` should be preserved as an input/debug artifact, but should not directly become the public final message for failed or warning cases, and should be diff-checked for success cases.\n- `edit_response_contract.py` and `edit_humanize.py` are likely touchpoints, but the implementation should preserve existing response contracts.\n- If narrative synthesis fails, the turn itself should not fail; use a deterministic fallback message.\n\n## Open Questions For The Planner\n\n- What exact state is available at response-construction time today, and what needs to be plumbed from validation/diagnostics?\n- Should the narrative LLM call be mandatory for all outcomes, or should clean simple successes use deterministic summaries unless complexity/warnings justify LLM synthesis?\n- What is the cheapest reliable model/provider path for the narrative call, and how should provider failures be isolated from edit success?\n- Where should message-quality checks live in the live agentic harness, and how strict should they be initially?\n\n## Constraints\n\n- Do not make final messaging able to change the graph or candidate.\n- Do not let message synthesis introduce a new hard failure mode for otherwise valid edits.\n- Do not expose internal gate jargon in the public message unless it is translated into user-actionable language.\n- Preserve existing public response fields unless there is a deliberate migration path.\n- Keep artifacts compact enough that normal turns do not become noisy or expensive.\n- Respect dirty worktree constraints; do not revert unrelated local changes.\n\n## Done Criteria\n\n- A new persisted narrative context artifact exists for agent-edit turns and includes at least: task, route/outcome, graph_changed, landed operations, rejected operations or diagnostics when available, validation/gate summary, selected precedent/plan summary if available, apply/queue eligibility, and recovery hint or warning data.\n- Final success messages are grounded in actual landed operations and do not claim no-op when operations landed.\n- Final failure/blocked/noop messages identify the phase/root cause and whether retry/user action is appropriate.\n- Deterministic contradiction checks prevent messages from claiming edits happened when `graph_changed=false`, claiming validation passed when validation failed, or asking no question for clarify outcomes.\n- Deterministic fallback messages cover success, clarify, noop, blocked, provider failure, malformed model response, validation failure, and refused emit.\n- Unit tests cover the contradiction guards and fallback behavior.\n- At least one existing live-agentic failure artifact is used as a regression fixture or equivalent test case for misleading executor narratives.\n- The standard relevant test suite passes.\n\n## Touchpoints\n\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/edit_batch_loop_intro.py`\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/edit_response_contract.py`\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/edit_humanize.py`\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/diagnostics.py`\n- `vibecomfy/comfy_nodes/agent/contracts.py`\n- `tests/live_agentic_harness/`\n- Existing turn artifacts under `out/editor_sessions/.../turns/...`\n\n## Anti-Scope\n\n- Do not solve all agentic eval failures in this sprint.\n- Do not rewrite the whole research-plan-execute architecture.\n- Do not add broad prompt instructions that make the executor more verbose instead of fixing final-message grounding.\n- Do not hide failures by making messages vague.\n\n## Prep Selection\n\nOverall plan difficulty: 4/5; selected profile: partnered-4; because the main risk is an architectural/message-contract plan that can pass ordinary tests while still producing misleading user-facing output.\n\nPlanning complexity: full; default critique/gate/review is appropriate because this crosses response construction, artifacts, and eval semantics.\n\nDepth: high; the planner needs to trace current state flow and design a clean integration without
